response.justification: Candidate is an experienced full-stack and web-focused engineer with broad polyglot exposure, but the resume shows no evidence of the hard requirements for this role: no native-level Japanese, no embedded firmware / LAN-switch development, no demonstrated C-for-UNIX or Linux kernel source modification work, and no security-protocol experience (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal). Listed C/C++ work is limited and dated (National Instruments, ~2000-2001), and the recent stack is TypeScript/Node/Dlang web and AI tooling — a poor match for an embedded networking security IC role.
strengths: Long, varied software career across web, mobile, desktop and some embedded-adjacent work
Some historical C/C++ exposure at National Instruments and Linux/Jack audio work
Comfortable with Linux environments, Docker, CI/CD and multiple languages
concerns: No native-level Japanese proficiency indicated (hard requirement)
No demonstrated embedded firmware, LAN-switch, or Linux kernel modification experience
No exposure to required security tech (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal) or switch networking (L2/L3, NETCONF, SNMP, chip SDKs)
